user_prompt: make windows or powershell compatible versions of `tools\code-style-format.sh` and `tools\code-style-check-format.sh`
agent_reply: They mirror the Bash scripts: scan `src`, `include`, `lib`, and `test` for `.c/.cpp/.h/.hpp`, then run `clang-format -i` or `clang-format --dry-run --Werror`.
I also added a clear error if `clang-format` is not on `PATH`. I verified the scripts execute under PowerShell; on this machine they stop at that expected missing-`clang-format` message.

user_prompt: add a way for me to specify an array of directories to ignore, such as `lib\arduino-liblame` and `lib\libhelix-esp32-arduino` and `lib\ShineWrapper\shine`
agent_reply: Done. I added configurable ignore arrays to all four `tools\code-style-*` scripts.
Shell scripts now have the same list as `ignore_dirs=(...)`, using forward slashes, and prune those dirs from `find`.
Verification: `git diff --check` passed. I did not run the formatter itself, to avoid creating a giant unrelated formatting churn while you’re still tuning `.clang-format`.

user_prompt: add to `tools\code-style-*` scripts a list of files to ignore, and add `src/sprites.cpp` and `src/version.cpp` to the list
agent_reply: Done. I added separate ignored-file lists to all four `tools/code-style-*` scripts:
I included `src/version.c` because that’s the file that actually exists, and kept `src/version.cpp` too since you named it.

Verification: `git diff --check` passed for the edited scripts. I couldn’t run the PowerShell check script itself because PowerShell is currently failing to spawn in the sandbox, but the script edits are straightforward and the search confirmed all four scripts now carry the same ignore list.
